chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Const. Petition No. D-2697 of 2024 Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) For hearing of CMA No.22819/2025 15th April, 2026. M/s Abdul Salam Memon and Ali Asadullah Bullo Advocates along with petitioners Imran Khan Jiskani, Shagufta Ahmed, Faisal Qureshi, Sanaullah Kalwar and Arshad Kahskheli. Mr. Malik Naeem Iqbal, Advocate for respondents along with respondents Muhammad Murad, M.Abbas Dayo and Abdul Wahid Channa. M/s Hakim Ali Shaikh, Addl.Advocate General Sindh and Sagheer Ahmed Abbasi, A.A.G.Sindh a/w Ghulam Ali Brahmani, Secretary (Services) SGA&CD and Sanaullah Qazi, Section Officer SGA&CD Karachi. >>>> <<<<< The core issue involved in the present petition is inter se seniority of Assistant Commissioners, appointed through initial appointment by Sindh Public Service Commission and by way of promotion from amongst Mukhtiarkars. The petitioners in the present lis of claim that initial appointment was made against quota reserved for Mukhtiarkars to be appointed by way of promotion in the year 2019. On the last date of hearing a report was filed by respondent No.2/Secretary (Services) SGA&CD Government of Sindh along with order passed by worthy Chief Minister Sindh, in which it transpired that 22 seats were available for appointment by way of promotion from amongst Mukhtiarkars. On the last date of hearing a report was filed by respondent No.2/Secretary (Services) SGA&CD Government of Sindh along with order passed by worthy Chief Minister Sindh, in which it transpired that 22 seats were available for appointment by way of promotion from amongst Mukhtiarkars. Today, respondent No.2/Secretary (Services) SGA&CD Government of Sindh is present in person and submitted a comprehensive report, which in our view is not in consonance with the concerns raised by the Court on previous date of hearing. 2 After arguing the matter to some length, Mr. Ghulam Ali Brahmani, Secretary (Services) SGA&CD Government of Sindh proposed that a committee may be constituted to examine the issues and furnish report so that grievance of parties may be redressed. Learned counsel for both the parties as well as parties present in court, agreed with such proposal. By consent of the parties the committee headed by Khalid Hyder Shah Sr. Member Board of Revenue, Government of Sindh and Bilal Ahmed Memon, Chairman CMIT and Noor Ahmed Samoo, Secretary Training Wing, Government of Sindh is constituted. The Committee shall hear and examine the case of parties. It will examine the background of the appointments in BS-17 and will decide the issue within twenty days’ time starting from tomorrow i.e. 16.04.2026. The Secretary (Services) SGA&CD Government of Sindh shall furnish entire record to the committee. The committee shall submit its report to this court on or before the next date of hearing. To come up on 06.05.2026. Let a copy of this order be facsimiled immediately to the Head and Members of the committee and Chief Secretary Government of Sindh for compliance. A copy of this order may also be delivered to learned Advocate General Sindh for onward communication to all concerned for compliance and correspondence. Learned MIT-II to ensure compliance. Judge Head of Const. Benches Aamir/PS Judge
